title: Postneonatal Respiratory Morbidity
authors: Raffay TM, Yildiz-Atar H, Kumar VHS, Lakshminrusimha S, Ryan RM / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Postneonatal Respiratory Morbidity?
เราคิดว่างานของเราจบเมื่อ discharge ทารกออก NICU — แต่ปอดของเด็กคนนั้นยังแบกภาระจากการเกิดก่อนกำหนดไปอีกหลายสิบปี. (1) PNPH (postneonatal pulmonary hypertension) พบใน 18–37% ของ BPD infants — mortality 33–48% ใน 2–3 ปี; underdiagnosed เพราะไม่มี symptom จนกว่าจะ stressed ด้วย RSV bronchiolitis หรือ surgery; (2) Childhood reactive airways disease ไม่ใช่ atopic asthma — pathomechanism ต่างกันสิ้นเชิง: preterm wheezing เกิดจาก oxygen + pressure exposure ไม่ใช่ allergy → treatment response ต่างกัน; (3) Adults born preterm มี FEV1 ต่ำกว่า term peers → early-onset COPD (Gough Eur Respir J 2014) — trajectory เริ่มต้นจากสิ่งที่เราทำใน NICU; (4) ทุก decision ใน NICU มี lifespan consequence: oxygen dose, days on ventilator, maternal smoking counsel, breast milk — ล้วนกำหนดปอดของผู้ใหญ่ในอนาคต. Barker DJ BMJ 1990: 'fetal origins of adult disease.'
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
นิยาม PNPH และแยกจาก PPHN (persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn) ได้อย่างชัดเจน
บอก prevalence ของ BPD-PH (18–37%), survival rates (Khemani 2007, del Cerro 2014), และ risk factors สำหรับ poor survival
อธิบาย pathogenesis ของ PNPH: arrest of lung development → ↓ alveolar number + rarefication of intra-acinar arteries → endothelial dysfunction → ↑ PAP → RV failure
ระบุ echocardiographic criteria ของ PNPH: TRJV >3 m/s, estimated systolic PAP >35 mmHg หรือ >50% systemic; RV dilation, IVS flattening
สรุป pulmonary vasodilator options ใน PNPH: sildenafil, tadalafil, bosentan, iloprost, treprostinil SQ — และ FDA warning ต่อ sildenafil ในเด็ก
อธิบาย pathophysiology ของ preterm reactive airways disease: airway smooth muscle hypertrophy, alveolar simplification, poor tethering → airway collapse
แยก wheezing phenotype ใน preterm (oxygen/pressure-driven) ออกจาก atopic asthma (allergy/genetics-driven)
ระบุ preventive strategies: palivizumab, CuZn-SOD, iNO, breastfeeding — และ evidence level ของแต่ละ intervention
อธิบาย long-term lung function trajectory ใน preterm survivors: ↓ FEV1, ↓ FEV1/FVC, ↓ DLCO → early-onset COPD (Simpson Lancet Child Adolesc Health 2018)
ระบุ prenatal + postnatal risk factors สำหรับ poor adult lung function ตาม Table 71.1: maternal smoking, air pollution, BPD, poor postnatal growth, no breast milk
วางแผน multidisciplinary follow-up schedule หลัง NICU discharge สำหรับ BPD-PH infant
1. Postneonatal Pulmonary Hypertension (PNPH)
1.1 นิยามและบริบท
PNPH: pulmonary hypertension ที่เกิดหลัง neonatal period ใน preterm survivors — แตกต่างจาก PPHN ซึ่งเป็น failure of transition to postnatal circulation ทันทีหลังเกิด
สาเหตุหลัก 4 กลุ่ม: (1) BPD (most common preterm cause); (2) Pulmonary hypoplasia (CDH, oligohydramnios); (3) FGR/SGA; (4) Congenital heart disease + genetic conditions (Trisomy 21)
Spectrum of PVD: ไม่ใช่ all-or-nothing — ตั้งแต่ mild PVD ที่ echo ปกติ จนถึง overt BPD-PH ที่ TRJV สูง; severe cases underrepresent true burden
Rates ไม่ลดใน 20 ปีที่ผ่านมา แม้ว่า respiratory management ดีขึ้นมาก: NICU survival ↑ → absolute number ของ BPD-PH ↑ (Stoll JAMA 2015)
1.2 Prevalence และ Survival Data

rows: BPD-PH in surfactant era; supra-systemic PAP = worst prognosis
del Cerro et al Pediatr Pulmonol 2014
Multi-center; factors: BPD severity, FGR, supra-systemic PAP
~67% survival; 77% weaned off vasodilators by median 2yr
Optimal vasodilator therapy case series; median weaning age 2yr
Israeli cohort; 40% weaned off Rx at median 3.2yr

blocks.text: BPD-PH associated outcomes vs BPD without PH: ↑ worse neurodevelopment, ↓ somatic growth, ↑ tracheostomy rate, ↑ O2 need, ↑ feeding problems, ↑ readmissions (Hansmann EPPVDN Pediatr Res 2021)
Long-term: school-age children born preterm → higher estimated PAP vs term peers; those with BPD > those without BPD (Zivanovic J Pediatr 2017)
Young adults born preterm → higher mean PAP on RHC vs term adults (Goss AJRCCM 2018) — but some 7–12yr cohorts show no difference → may resolve in some children
1.3 Pathogenesis

Pathogenesis ของ PNPH — Cascade ของ Arrest
Extreme preterm birth → arrest of lung development → ↓ alveolar number + rarefication of intra-acinar arteries. ↑ compounded by: alveolar hypoxia, hypercarbia, hyperoxic stress → endothelial dysfunction + PA smooth muscle proliferation. Disrupted vascular growth + abnormal growth factor signaling → impaired lung structure → ↓ alveolar-capillary surface area + ↓ PBF + ↑ airway reactivity + V/Q mismatch + ↓ gas exchange. Long-term: exercise intolerance, respiratory decompensation with acute illness, ↑ PAP → RV dysfunction. Key: supplemental oxygen alone → alveolar simplification + affects heart and kidney development (Kumar Anat Rec 2018).
1.4 Monitoring และ Echocardiography
SpO2 targets: ATS/AHA guidelines 2015: ≥92–95% ใน PNPH; European guidelines: ≥95% for confirmed pediatric PH — ยัง unresolved สำหรับ PNPH specifically
Echo criteria ของ PH: TRJV >3 m/s + estimated systolic PAP >35 mmHg หรือ >50% systemic; แต่ absence of measurable TRJV ≠ rule out PH
Other echo findings: RV dilation, RV hypertrophy, IVS flattening, PA dilation, RA enlargement — ใช้ร่วมกันในการ support diagnosis

Serial echo: indicated สำหรับ monitor disease progression/resolution; cardiac catheterization เมื่อ: suboptimal response, 2nd vasodilator being considered, concern for pulmonary vein stenosis, or echo alone insufficient
Acute vasoreactivity testing: 100% O2 ± iNO during cardiac cath → aid prognostication + therapy optimization (Frank J Pediatr 2019)
NT-pro-BNP: serial monitoring ร่วมกับ echo อาจมีประโยชน์ในการ assess BPD-PH progression — แต่ยังเป็น expert opinion; multicenter trials needed
Nutrition: preterm infants fed high volumes of mother's milk → better cardiac function + pulmonary hemodynamics at 1yr (El-Khuffash JAMA Netw Open 2021)
1.5 Pulmonary Vasodilator Treatment

rows: FDA warning: ↑ mortality in pediatric PAH (STARTS-2); use with caution; still widely used
Less studied in infants; once-daily dosing advantage
Used when suboptimal response to oral agents; injection site pain

blocks.text: Caution: vasodilators may ↑ pulmonary edema in: pulmonary venous obstruction, LV dysfunction, cardiac shunts, systemic-pulmonary collaterals → ↑ risk (Lakshminrusimha J Pediatr 2020; Chandrasekharan Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2021)
Severe refractory PNPH: RV failure → surgical options: atrial septostomy or reverse Potts shunt (Grady Pediatr Pulmonol 2021); last resort: lung transplant (Liu Am J Surg Pathol 2020)
Team approach: multidisciplinary team — neonatal intensivists + pediatric pulmonology + pediatric cardiology; inpatient + outpatient; ล้วน provide continuity of care ตั้งแต่ NICU discharge
2. Reactive Airways Disease ในเด็กที่เกิดก่อนกำหนด
2.1 Overview และการแยกจาก Atopic Asthma

Preterm Wheezing ≠ Atopic Asthma — แยก Pathomechanism ให้ได้
Preterm-associated reactive airways disease: strongly associated with ↑ supplemental O2 exposure + positive pressure ventilation + ↓ GA — oxygen/pressure-driven. Atopic asthma (term infants): driven by genetic inheritance, allergy, environment, inflammation — not oxygen exposure. Clinical implication: classic allergen avoidance + atopic workup มีประโยชน์น้อยใน preterm wheezer; response to inhaled steroids และ bronchodilators variable; treatment must be individualized. Even moderate-to-late preterm → ↑ risk for expiratory flow restriction (Thunqvist Pediatr Pulmonol 2018).
2.2 Pathophysiology
Airway smooth muscle hypertrophy: coupled to alveolar simplification → poorly tethered intrapulmonary airways → ↑ airway collapse + ↑ airway resistance at baseline + during bronchoconstriction

Oxygen dose-response: 40–60% O2 causes greatest ↑ airway smooth muscle + airway hyperreactivity to methacholine (Dylag Pediatr Res 2020; Onugha Neonatology 2015) — paradoxically >70% O2 → ↑ baseline airway resistance + more alveolar simplification + ↓ hyperreactivity (blunted by apoptosis)
Human fetal airway smooth muscle: grown at 60% O2 → ↑ proliferation + hyperresponsive to cholinergic/histamine; >60% → ↑ apoptosis + blunted response (Hartman Am J Physiol 2012)
CPAP stretch effect: pressure effects of CPAP or in vitro smooth muscle stretch → predispose to alveolar simplification + functional airway reactivity (Mayer Pediatr Res 2022)

Additional risk factors: maternal tobacco, chorioamnionitis, postnatal RSV/viral infection, air pollution — all well described to associate with childhood wheezing (Lodge J Pediatr 2014; Bobolea J Investig Allergol 2019)
2.3 Clinical Outcomes
PROP trial (Ryan J Pediatr 2019): >50% ของ preterm survivors <29 wk → ≥1 postdischarge respiratory medication ใน first year of life; most commonly inhaled bronchodilator

Kotecha et al Am J Epidemiol 2019: serial follow-up 9mo–11yr: preterm-born → higher rates of early + persistent wheeze vs term peers; large proportion = early wheeze ที่ resolve with time (encouraging)
Di Fiore et al J Perinatol 2019: early inspired O2 + intermittent hypoxemia in extremely premature → associated with asthma medication use at 2yr
Dylag et al J Pediatr 2020: early neonatal O2 exposure predicts pulmonary morbidity + functional deficits at 1yr
Poorest trajectory: home tobacco exposure + earlier GA + family history asthma + longer O2 exposure + mechanical ventilation → worst long-term wheeze trajectory
2.4 Prevention
Palivizumab (RSV prophylaxis): ↓ incidence of recurrent wheezing (but NOT atopic asthma) in preterm infants (Mochizuki AJRCCM 2017) — class: RCT evidence
Intratracheal CuZn-SOD (recombinant human): ↓ prescribed asthma medication at 1yr vs placebo (Davis Pediatrics 2003) — RCT; addresses oxidative stress mechanism
iNO (inhaled nitric oxide): ↓ asthma medication use at 1yr (Hibbs J Pediatr 2008) — RCT; anti-inflammatory + vasodilatory mechanism
Breastfeeding: prospective birth cohort >10,000 infants: protective of wheezing in preterm group except late-onset wheeze after 7yr (Kotecha 2019)
Noninvasive ventilation: despite ↑ use of nasal ventilation, no significant ↓ in O2 dependence at 36wk + no improvement in childhood lung function (Doyle NEJM 2017) — sobering finding
3. Adult Lung Function Impairment — Early-Onset COPD
3.1 Concept: Lung Function Trajectories
ปอดปกติ: growth ตั้งแต่ in utero → plateau ใน early adulthood (~20yr) → gradual decline with age (Line A, Fig 71.3)
Preterm without BPD (Line B): peak lung function ต่ำกว่า term peers ตั้งแต่ early life → decline at same rate → reach COPD threshold เร็วกว่า
Preterm with BPD (Line C): further ↓ peak + faster decline → COPD ใน 3rd–4th decade; factors: maternal smoking, air pollution, nutrition, genetics
Early life risk factors track lifetime: poor lung functional trajectories from childhood to old age = important risk for COPD (Sly Bush AJRCCM 2016; McGrath-Morrow Ther Adv Respir Dis 2019)
3.2 Clinical Evidence
Gough et al Eur Respir J 2014: adults with BPD as infants → significantly lower FEV1 + FEF25–75% in 3rd–4th decade → clinically important airflow obstruction
Yang et al AJRCCM 2022: VLBW infants as adults → ↓ FEV1, FEV1/FVC, DLCO, ↓ exercise capacity, altered cardiac structure/function — population-based cohort
Clemm et al Ann Am Thorac Soc 2014: exercise capacity ↓ after extremely preterm birth; develops adolescence → adulthood
Vrijlandt et al Respir Res 2005: 19yr-old adults born preterm: females at higher risk for respiratory symptoms than males
Emphysema: reported in young adult survivors of moderate/severe BPD on CT chest (Wong J Med Imaging 2011) — เป็น COPD subtype ที่เป็น hallmark ของ preterm lung disease
Ventilatory limitation to exercise: similar to COPD patients; critical constraint to tidal volume expansion → reduced exercise tolerance, dyspnea, leg discomfort (Lovering Ann Am Thorac Soc 2014)
Lange et al NEJM 2015: low FEV1 ใน early adulthood = significant risk factor for COPD development — pathways ที่เริ่มจาก preterm birth
3.3 Risk Factors (Table 71.1)

rows: Failure to provide mother's own milk (MoM)

blocks.text: Maternal smoking: ↑ odds BPD + late respiratory morbidities; nicotine affects multiple organ systems with lifelong consequences (Maritz Int J Environ Res 2011)

Air pollution: high maternal PM exposure → adverse lung development (Hsu AJRCCM 2015); even low-to-moderate air pollution impairs lung function significantly more in preterm vs term (Decrue AJRCCM 2022 — BILD study)
Vitamin C supplementation: to pregnant smokers → ↓ impact of maternal smoking on offspring lung function via epigenetic mechanism (Shorey-Kendrick Clin Epigenetics 2021) — potential intervention
3.4 Prevention Strategy
Optimize lung health ใน NICU: oxygen targets, ↓ days on ventilator, nutrition optimization, prevent postnatal infections, breast milk preference
Counsel mothers: ↓ tobacco before/during pregnancy; passive smoke exposure reduction — major challenge but huge long-term impact
Address air pollution: particularly acute ใน rapidly industrializing countries; community-level interventions needed
Address nutrition in pregnancy: ↓ FGR + low birth weight → ↑ peak lung volume
Routine screening: spirometry + DLCO in adult former preterm patients; elicit birth history (GA, BW, NICU course, respiratory treatments) to identify at-risk adults for early COPD
4. Algorithm — PNPH Monitoring หลัง NICU Discharge
4.1 Follow-Up Framework

rows: SpO2, baseline echo, NT-pro-BNP, growth assessment
Growth, O2 needs, respiratory symptoms, echo
Serial echo, NT-pro-BNP, lung function if feasible
Wean vasodilator if stable; RSV prophylaxis
SpO2 at home + ER presentation with bronchiolitis/surgery
If hypoxemia or ↑ work of breathing
PH may unmask during stress — echo urgently

blocks.text: SpO2 <92% in room air (incl. night/sleep) → supplement O2 — ATS/AHA guideline 2015
Cardiac catheterization indications: suboptimal response to therapy, 2nd vasodilator needed, suspected pulmonary vein stenosis, or echo insufficient to explain picture
Team composition: neonatal intensivist + pediatric pulmonology + pediatric cardiology — inpatient AND outpatient continuity is critical
5. NICU Practical — ปกป้องปอดระยะยาว
5.1 Oxygen Stewardship ตั้งแต่ Day 1
40–60% O2 = worst zone: causes greatest airway smooth muscle deposition + airway hyperreactivity (Dylag 2020) — ไม่ใช่ยิ่งสูงยิ่งแย่; ช่วง moderate hyperoxia คือ critical window
Target SpO2 91–95%: avoid routine hyperoxia; cumulative O2 exposure ใน first days → predicts asthma medication use at 2yr (Di Fiore J Perinatol 2019) + pulmonary morbidity at 1yr
Document cumulative O2 exposure time + FiO2 as routine variable ใน chart — ข้อมูลนี้มีความหมายต่อผู้ดูแลใน follow-up clinic
5.2 Breast Milk — Pulmonary Vascular Benefit
El-Khuffash JAMA Netw Open 2021: high volume of mother's milk → better cardiac function + pulmonary hemodynamics at 1yr follow-up ใน preterm infants
Counsel families เรื่อง breast milk ไม่ใช่แค่เรื่อง NEC/infection prevention — แต่ยังเป็นเรื่อง pulmonary vascular health ระยะยาว
5.3 Maternal Smoking Counseling ใน NICU
Nicotine crosses placenta + breast milk → affects airway development; even antenatal exposure → ↑ BPD odds + late respiratory morbidities
Vitamin C supplementation: in pregnant smokers → ↓ impact on offspring lung function (Shorey-Kendrick 2021) — แจ้ง OB team เมื่อ identified
Postnatal passive smoke (home exposure) = major modifiable risk for poor lung trajectory — discuss at every follow-up visit
5.4 RSV Prophylaxis Decision
Palivizumab: reduces recurrent wheezing (not atopic asthma) in preterm (Mochizuki AJRCCM 2017); Thai guideline: <35 wk with significant CLD หรือ CHD within first RSV season
ระบุ BPD severity ก่อน discharge → inform RSV prophylaxis decision; document respiratory medication plan
5.5 Discharge Planning สำหรับ BPD-PH
ตรวจ echo ก่อน discharge ทุก significant BPD patient: TRJV, RV size, IVS pattern
Start home O2 if SpO2 <92% room air (incl. sleep) — send home O2 saturation monitor
Arrange 1-month follow-up with pulmonary hypertension team: echo + NT-pro-BNP + growth + feeding assessment
Family education: recognize signs of worsening PH — ↑ work of breathing, ↓ feeding, diaphoresis, syncope → ER evaluation
6. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — PNPH อาจ Unmask ระหว่าง RSV Bronchiolitis หรือ Surgery
Preterm infant ที่ discharge ออกไปโดยไม่มี sign of PH อาจ present กลับมา ER ด้วย hypoxemia + RV failure ระหว่าง intercurrent illness (RSV bronchiolitis) หรือ elective procedure (gastrostomy, hernia repair). Mechanism: stress → ↑ hypoxia → ↑ PAP → unmasked PH. สำคัญ: ส่ง echo ทุก BPD infant ที่ hypoxemia disproportionate to apparent respiratory illness severity. TRJV >3 m/s + estimated systolic PAP >35 mmHg → confirm BPD-PH → ปรึกษา pulmonary hypertension team ทันที.

PEARL 2 — 40–60% O2 คือ Worst Zone สำหรับ Airway Development
ความเชื่อเดิม: ยิ่งลด O2 ยิ่งดี — ถูก. แต่ที่แย่ที่สุดสำหรับ airway smooth muscle hypertrophy + hyperreactivity ไม่ใช่ 80–100% O2 แต่เป็น moderate hyperoxia 40–60% (Dylag 2020; Onugha 2015). สาเหตุ: high FiO2 → ↑ cell apoptosis → blunted smooth muscle response; moderate FiO2 → ↑ proliferation + contractility. Clinical pearl: SpO2 targets 91–95% ไม่ใช่แค่เพื่อ ROP — แต่เพื่อ airway development. Cumulative O2 exposure ใน first days of NICU life predicts asthma medication use at 2yr.

PEARL 3 — Adults Born Preterm มี 'COPD without Smoking History'
Former VLBW/BPD patients ใน 30s–40s → present to pulmonologist ด้วย dyspnea on exertion, FEV1/FVC <0.70, emphysema on CT — ไม่เคยสูบบุหรี่. ถ้าไม่ถาม birth history (GA, BW, NICU course) → missed diagnosis. Mechanism: lung function trajectory starts low (↓ peak FEV1) + declines at normal rate → COPD threshold reached decades earlier than average. Key message: เวลาที่ neonatologist care for preterm infant ใน NICU คือ window ที่สำคัญที่สุดในการป้องกัน COPD ในอนาคต.

PEARL 4 — Sildenafil FDA Warning ใน Pediatric PH
FDA ออก warning เกี่ยวกับ use of sildenafil ใน pediatric PAH หลัง STARTS-2 trial (Barst Circulation 2014) แสดง ↑ mortality ใน high-dose sildenafil group. แต่ sildenafil ยังถูกใช้อย่างกว้างขวางสำหรับ BPD-PH ใน low-to-moderate dose เนื่องจากขาด alternatives ที่ดีกว่า. Key message: (1) ใช้ lowest effective dose; (2) ไม่ใช้ high-dose monotherapy; (3) ปรึกษา pediatric PH specialist; (4) อธิบาย FDA warning แก่ family อย่างชัดเจนก่อน start therapy. Tadalafil (once-daily PDE5-i) เป็น alternative แต่ data ใน infants ยังน้อย.

PEARL 5 — BPD-PH Weaning Off Vasodilators: Median Age 2 Years
ข้อมูลที่น่าหวัง: ใน case series ที่ optimal pulmonary vasodilator therapy, nearly 77% ของ BPD-PH patients ที่ discharge กลับบ้านบน vasodilator → weaned off medications ที่ median age 2yr (Nees Children 2020). Israeli cohort: 95% survival at 2yr, 40% weaned off at median 3.2yr. Key implication: (1) BPD-PH ไม่จำเป็นต้องเป็น lifelong therapy; (2) serial echo + NT-pro-BNP → guide weaning decisions; (3) family counseling: ไม่ต้องยา lifelong แต่ต้อง close follow-up. Randomized trials ยังขาดอยู่ — guidelines ยังเป็น expert opinion.
7. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Barker DJ BMJ 1990;301:1111: fetal origins of adult disease — foundational concept; perinatal factors impact long-term organ function including lung
Khemani E et al Pediatrics 2007;120:1260: BPD-PH in surfactant era: survival 61% at 1yr, 52% at 2–3yr; supra-systemic PAP + FGR + severe BPD = poor prognosis
del Cerro MJ et al Pediatr Pulmonol 2014;49:49: multi-center BPD-PH cohort: 77% survival 1yr, 66% at 2–3yr after diagnosis
Nees SN et al Children 2020;7:97: targeted therapy BPD-PH: 67% survival; 77% weaned off vasodilators median 2yr — optimal therapy case series
Abman SH et al Circulation 2015;132:2037: AHA/ATS pediatric PH guidelines: SpO2 target 92–95%; echo criteria; vasodilator recommendations
El-Khuffash A et al JAMA Netw Open 2021;4:e2121206: high maternal breast milk volumes → better cardiac function + pulmonary hemodynamics at 1yr in preterm infants
Dylag AM et al Pediatr Res 2020;87:1201: pulmonary mechanics + structural lung development after neonatal hyperoxia in mice; 40–60% O2 = worst zone for airway smooth muscle
Onugha H et al Neonatology 2015;108:65: airway hyperreactivity delayed after mild neonatal hyperoxia — dose-response curves for O2 exposure in neonatal models
Ryan RM et al J Pediatr 2019;208:148 (PROP): >50% of preterm <29wk → ≥1 respiratory medication in year 1; numerous postdischarge respiratory morbidities
Kotecha SJ et al Am J Epidemiol 2019;188:527: comparison of early-life risk factors for wheeze in preterm vs term; serial follow-up 9mo–11yr; large proportion early wheeze resolves
Mochizuki H et al AJRCCM 2017;196:29: palivizumab prophylaxis in preterm → ↓ recurrent wheezing but NOT atopic asthma; 6yr follow-up
Davis JM et al Pediatrics 2003;111:469: intratracheal CuZn-SOD → ↓ prescribed asthma medication at 1yr vs placebo — RCT addressing oxidative mechanism
Hibbs AM et al J Pediatr 2008;153:525: iNO in preterm → ↓ asthma medication at 1yr vs placebo — RCT (NO to prevent chronic lung disease trial)

Simpson SJ et al Lancet Child Adolesc Health 2018;2:350: lung function trajectories throughout childhood in very preterm survivors: impaired trajectory; poorest = tobacco + lower GA + longer O2 + ventilation
Gough A et al Eur Respir J 2014;43:808: adults with BPD as infants: significantly lower FEV1 + FEF25–75% in 3rd–4th decade — clinically substantial airflow obstruction
Yang J et al AJRCCM 2022;205:88: VLBW as adults: ↓ FEV1, FEV1/FVC, DLCO, ↓ exercise capacity, altered cardiac structure/function — population-based
Decrue F et al AJRCCM 2022;205:99 (BILD): air pollution impacts lung function significantly more in preterm vs term infants — even low-to-moderate PM exposure
Lange P et al NEJM 2015;373:111: lung function trajectories to COPD: low FEV1 in early adulthood = significant COPD risk — pathway from preterm birth
Doyle LW et al NEJM 2017;377:329: despite ↑ noninvasive ventilation: no significant ↓ O2 dependence at 36wk, no improvement in childhood lung function at 8yr
Stoll BJ et al JAMA 2015;314:1039: NICHD NRN: trends 1993–2012: rates of BPD and PH have NOT decreased despite improved care — absolute burden ↑ with ↑ survival
8. Self-Check Q&A
Q: แยก PNPH ออกจาก PPHN ได้อย่างไร และสาเหตุหลักของ PNPH ในทารกคลอดก่อนกำหนดคืออะไร?

ตอบ: PPHN: failure of transition to postnatal circulation after birth — เกิดในช่วง immediate neonatal period. PNPH: pulmonary hypertension ที่เกิดหลัง neonatal period ใน survivors — chronic, progressive, related to lung development arrest. สาเหตุหลักของ PNPH: BPD (most common in preterm); pulmonary hypoplasia (CDH, oligohydramnios); FGR/SGA; congenital heart disease/trisomy 21. Pathogenesis: extreme preterm → ↓ alveolar number + rarefication of intra-acinar arteries → endothelial dysfunction → ↑ PAP → RV failure.
Q: Echo criteria ที่ใช้ define PNPH คืออะไร และ echo findings อื่นๆ ที่ support diagnosis?

ตอบ: Primary criterion: TRJV >3 m/s + estimated systolic PAP >35 mmHg หรือ >50% systemic systolic pressure. สำคัญ: absence of measurable TRJV ≠ rule out PH. Supporting findings: RV dilation, RV hypertrophy, IVS flattening, PA dilation, RA enlargement. Serial echo: monitor progression or resolution. Cardiac cath: when response suboptimal, 2nd vasodilator considered, or pulmonary vein stenosis suspected.
Q: SpO2 target ใน PNPH คืออะไร และ debate ที่ยังไม่ resolved คืออะไร?

ตอบ: ATS/AHA 2015: recommend SpO2 92–95% (supplement O2 if <92% in room air including sleep). European guideline: suggest 95% for confirmed pediatric PH. Unresolved debate: neither target has been studied systematically in PNPH specifically — ยังเป็น crucial research gap. Optimal target SpO2 in PNPH remains to be determined by multicenter trial. Clinical practice: use ATS/AHA 92–95% target until better evidence available.
Q: พยาธิกำเนิดของ preterm reactive airways disease แตกต่างจาก atopic asthma อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Preterm reactive airways disease: strongly associated with supplemental O2 + positive pressure + ↓ GA (Dylag 2020; Di Fiore 2019) — oxygen/pressure-driven. Mechanism: airway smooth muscle hypertrophy + alveolar simplification → poorly tethered airways → ↑ collapse + ↑ resistance. Atopic asthma (term): driven by allergy/genetics/environment/inflammation — NOT oxygen exposure. Clinical implication: response to inhaled steroids + bronchodilators is variable ใน preterm wheeze; allergen avoidance มีประโยชน์น้อยกว่า; treatment must be individualized.
Q: O2 dose-response สำหรับ airway smooth muscle development: ทำไม 40–60% ถึงแย่ที่สุด?

ตอบ: 40–60% O2: causes greatest ↑ airway smooth muscle deposition + airway hyperreactivity to methacholine (Dylag Pediatr Res 2020; Onugha 2015). Mechanism: moderate hyperoxia → ↑ smooth muscle cell proliferation + ↑ contractility + ↑ cholinergic/histamine responsiveness (Hartman Am J Physiol 2012). High O2 (>70%): → ↑ apoptosis → blunted smooth muscle response + ↑ baseline resistance + more alveolar simplification + ↑ elastin = BPD hallmarks (less hyperreactivity). Clinical lesson: avoid prolonged moderate hyperoxia; SpO2 target 91–95% protects airway development.
Q: Palivizumab ช่วยอะไรในเด็กที่เกิดก่อนกำหนด? ลด atopic asthma ด้วยหรือไม่?

ตอบ: Palivizumab ลด: incidence of recurrent wheezing ใน preterm infants (Mochizuki AJRCCM 2017; 6yr follow-up). Palivizumab ไม่ลด: atopic asthma — confirms that preterm wheezing phenotype ≠ atopic asthma; mechanism of RSV wheezing ≠ mechanism of atopy. Other interventions that ↓ respiratory medication: intratracheal CuZn-SOD (Davis 2003) + iNO (Hibbs 2008) — both RCTs showing ↓ prescribed asthma medication at 1yr vs placebo.
Q: อธิบาย lung function trajectory ใน adult survivors of preterm birth และ risk ของ early-onset COPD

ตอบ: Line A (term): peak ~20yr, gradual decline. Line B (preterm without BPD): ↓ peak FEV1 → decline at same rate → reaches COPD threshold earlier. Line C (preterm with BPD): further ↓ peak + faster decline → COPD in 3rd–4th decade. Evidence: Gough 2014 (↓ FEV1/FEF25-75% in adults), Yang 2022 (VLBW → ↓ FEV1/FVC/DLCO + ↓ exercise capacity), Wong 2011 (emphysema on CT in young BPD adults). Key risk factor: low FEV1 in early adulthood = significant COPD risk (Lange NEJM 2015). Screen with spirometry + elicit birth history.
Q: Sildenafil FDA warning: ควรใช้ยังไงใน PNPH?

ตอบ: STARTS-2 trial (Barst Circulation 2014): high-dose sildenafil monotherapy → ↑ mortality in pediatric PAH → FDA warning against sildenafil use in children with PAH. However: sildenafil still widely used in BPD-PH in low-to-moderate dose เนื่องจาก evidence base สำหรับ alternatives น้อยกว่า. Practice: (1) lowest effective dose; (2) avoid high-dose monotherapy; (3) consult pediatric PH specialist; (4) document FDA warning discussion with family; (5) RCTs needed — guidelines still expert opinion.
Q: Prenatal and postnatal factors ที่ modifiable เพื่อ prevent adult COPD ใน former preterm infants มีอะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: Prenatal: (1) maternal smoking cessation — vitamin C supplementation in smokers → ↓ fetal lung epigenetic damage (Shorey-Kendrick 2021); (2) ↓ air pollution exposure; (3) prevent FGR via nutrition + UTI treatment + placental health. Postnatal: (1) optimal O2 targets (91–95%); (2) minimize ventilator days; (3) maximize mother's own milk; (4) prevent postnatal infections + RSV prophylaxis; (5) promote breastfeeding (Kotecha 2019); (6) prohibit passive smoke in home. Long-term: screen with spirometry; ensure pediatric PH team follow-up; adult medicine handoff with clear NICU history.
Q: BPD-PH infant ที่ discharge กลับบ้านต้องมี follow-up plan อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Before discharge: baseline echo (TRJV, RV size, IVS), NT-pro-BNP, SpO2 (resting + sleep), growth assessment. Start O2 if SpO2 <92% room air. 1 month: SpO2 monitoring (incl. overnight), feeding assessment, echo ถ้า symptoms. 3 months: routine echo + growth; consider vasodilator weaning if stable. 6–12 months: serial echo + NT-pro-BNP; lung function if feasible; adjust vasodilator; RSV prophylaxis decision. Acute illness: echo ทันทีถ้า hypoxemia disproportionate to severity of illness. Median weaning age: 2yr (Nees 2020); 95% survival in Israeli cohort (Kadmon 2017).

Day 169 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.72
บทต่อไป: Chapter 72 — Cardiac Embryology. เตรียมตัว: heart tube formation, cardiac looping (D-loop), septation ของ atria/ventricles/outflow tract; transcription factors (NKX2.5, GATA4, TBX5, TBX1) ที่ drive cardiac development; second heart field vs first heart field; neural crest contribution to outflow tract; กลไกของ congenital heart defects: VSD, ASD, AVSD (trisomy 21), conotruncal defects (22q11); ทำความเข้าใจ embryology = เข้าใจ why each CHD has its specific anatomy + associated defects.
